shared_properties: Side-profile T-Rex body components: head, torso, forearms, hind legs, tail.
differences: Body height reduces from 94 px to 60 px.
Snout extends forward within a 118 px width envelope.
Leg rhythm stays at 12 fps to preserve gameplay timing.

context.usage: Used when down input is active and a low pterodactyl is within the dodge window.

shape.silhouette: Side-profile T-Rex silhouette with 2px outline, single-body fill, and background cutout for eye and mouth details.

acceptance_criteria: Asset canvas is exactly 118x60 px per frame.
Primary body fill remains #535353 and background cutout stays transparent.
Leg, tail, and head pixels stay aligned to whole-pixel positions with no anti-aliasing.

reference_images.label: player_dino_reference
reference_images.purpose: Player silhouette, jump posture, and run loop proportion anchor.

reference_images.label: pixel_art_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Pixel-art outline, low-detail silhouette, and monochrome contrast anchor.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteSheetBundle for player states so animation frames share one logical entity per state.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load character/player_dino_duck_2f_12fps.png through AssetServer and register the handle under player_dino_duck_2f_12fps.
runtime_notes: Keep source pixels unfiltered with nearest-neighbor sampling and integer world transforms.
Spawn order follows execution_order so downstream setup can validate deterministic asset availability.
Collision and UI code read logical dimensions from specs.dimensions_px rather than texture metadata.
